title: narcissus leonensis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Narcissus leonensis subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western form of Leon's daffodil or Narcissus leonensis, is a plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family and is endemic to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Narcissus leonensis subsp. occidentalis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
